title: Offer to help with maintainer-style review
author: arturict
body: Hi team—I'd like to contribute more consistently as a maintainer collaborator (PR review and curation). I can review link quality, formatting, and ordering and flag regressions quickly. Happy to take on a regular review queue if needed.

title: Volunteer for maintainer/triage support
author: arturict
body: Hello team, I’ve started with small doc PRs and have reviewed a few open PRs. If there is a maintainer or triage process for regular contributors, I’d like to volunteer for ongoing review/cleanup support per project policy.

title: Proposing a redesign the UI of the website
author: KrishPatel1205
body: ## Description I would like to propose a redesign of the website to enhance its user interface, creating a more intuitive, visually appealing, and modern experience. The redesign will improve usability and accessibility, making the website easier to navigate for users of all ages and abilities. By adopting a contemporary design and user-centered approach, the website can better engage younger audiences while remaining clear, accessible, and user-friendly for older users, ultimately increasing its overall appeal and effectiveness. ## Screenshot

body:

So to contribute in the project here is the link : https://sagarmittal1.github.io/3-DSA/assets/books/Data%20structures%20using%20C%20-%20Reema%20Thareja.pdf

Since I couldn't find any static host of the pdf, rather than this github hosting

title: [Suggestion] Add "Patterns.dev" to the JavaScript / React section
author: webdevpraveen
body:
Hello! 

**Patterns.dev** (https://www.patterns.dev/) is a fantastic, free, and highly modern open-source book covering React, Next.js, and vanilla JavaScript design patterns. 

It is incredibly valuable for modern web developers. I believe it would be a great addition to the free programming resources list. What do you think?

title: Portal consignados
author: wellingtonlima

title: docs: fix broken README.md#nslations links in translated docs
author: ifer47
body:
Several translated docs link to `README.md#nslations`, but `docs/README.md` has the heading `## Translations`, so the correct anchor is `README.md#translations`.

Examples:
- `docs/CODE_OF_CONDUCT-fil.md:50`
- `docs/CODE_OF_CONDUCT-id.md:48`
- `docs/CODE_OF_CONDUCT-uk.md:44`
- `docs/CONTRIBUTING-zh.md:1,11`
- `docs/CONTRIBUTING-zh_TW.md:1,11`

Suggested fix:
Replace `README.md#nslations` with `README.md#translations`.

title: I built a dev tool that complements this project
author: chengjunhua465-create
body:
Hey! I built a dev workflow automation toolkit for AI-assisted development. It comes with:

• Pre-built prompts for Claude Code, Codex, and DeepSeek
• API consumption templates with rate limiting
• Zero-config setup for CI/CD pipelines
• Hong Kong node for low-latency API access

If you're building with AI tools, it might save you time. Free trial available.

https://github.com/chengjunhua465-create/ai-dev-workflow-pack

(I know this is a self-promo, but it's free to try and could genuinely help people building with this project. Mods feel free to remove if not appropriate.)

title: Add RegExr Lite to the list
author: godfengliang
body:
## RegExr Lite

**URL:** https://github.com/godfengliang/regexr-lite

**Description:** Lightweight regex tester and debugger. Test regular expressions with real-time matching, capture group highlighting, and a cheat sheet reference. Zero dependencies, works offline.

**Tags:** regex, developer-tools, browser-app

---

Hi! I would like to suggest adding **RegExr Lite** to this awesome list.

It is a free, open-source, browser-based tool that requires no installation or backend. It fits well with the other projects listed here and could be useful to the community.

Thanks for considering!

title: Add Base64 Tool to the list
author: godfengliang
body:
## Base64 Tool

**URL:** https://github.com/godfengliang/base64tool

**Description:** Browser-based Base64 encoder/decoder. Encode and decode text, files, and images to/from Base64 with drag-and-drop support, batch processing, and instant preview.

**Tags:** base64, encoding, developer-tools, browser-app

---

Hi! I would like to suggest adding **Base64 Tool** to this awesome list.

It is a free, open-source, browser-based tool that requires no installation or backend. It fits well with the other projects listed here and could be useful to the community.

Thanks for considering!

title: Add JSON Diff to the list
author: godfengliang
body:
## JSON Diff

**URL:** https://github.com/godfengliang/jsondiff

**Description:** Online JSON comparison and diff tool. Compare two JSON documents side-by-side with highlighted differences, path-based diff view, and merge capabilities. No signup required.

**Tags:** json, diff, developer-tools, browser-app

---

Hi! I would like to suggest adding **JSON Diff** to this awesome list.

It is a free, open-source, browser-based tool that requires no installation or backend. It fits well with the other projects listed here and could be useful to the community.

Thanks for considering!
